skill.name: architecture-patterns
skill.description: Implement proven backend architecture patterns including Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design. Use when architecting complex backend systems or refactoring existing applications for better maintainability.
skill.summary: Implement proven backend architecture patterns including Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture,...
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 29 potential issues in SKILL.md, including external command, environment access, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, architecture vocabulary, parameterized SQL examples, and illustrative API-key dependency injection. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable installer, network beacon, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Backticks
low_findings.description: Static external command detections map to Markdown code fences and sample code block boundaries, not Ruby or shell execution. The file is documentation and does not define executable skill commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are literal Markdown triple-backtick delimiters. No shell command, command substitution, or runtime execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Illustrative API Key Parameter
low_findings.description: The environment-access alerts refer to an example Stripe adapter constructor and assignment. The example receives api_key as a parameter and does not read process environment variables or secrets from the host.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code sample shows api_key as a function argument. There is no process.env, os.environ, filesystem secret read, or outbound exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Architecture Terms And Identifiers
low_findings.description: Weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match words such as Clean Architecture, Domain-Driven Design, id fields, customer identifiers, and SQL examples. These are explanatory architecture examples, not cryptographic or host reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is ordinary architecture guidance and sample domain model code. No weak algorithm call, host inspection command, or system inventory collection appears.

content.value_statement: Backend systems often become hard to test, change, and explain as business logic spreads across frameworks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains Clean Architecture layers and inward dependency rules.
Shows ports and adapters for database, payment, and notification boundaries.
Demonstrates DDD entities, value objects, aggregates, repositories, and domain events.
Provides sample Python structure for use cases, repositories, controllers, and adapters.
Lists practical best practices and pitfalls for backend architecture decisions.
limitations: The examples are illustrative and are not a complete working application.
It does not include the referenced resource files or asset templates in this package.
It focuses on backend structure, not deployment, observability, or infrastructure automation.
Teams must adapt boundaries and abstractions to their domain complexity.

use_cases.title: Plan A New Service Boundary
use_cases.description: Use the patterns to define domain models, use cases, ports, and adapters before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Refactor Framework-Coupled Code
use_cases.description: Move business rules out of controllers and persistence models into testable domain and use-case layers.

use_cases.title: Create Architecture Standards
use_cases.description: Use the terminology and examples to align teams on dependency direction, boundaries, and repository contracts.

prompt_templates.title: Explain The Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design for a backend team. Compare when each pattern is useful.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a backend service using Clean Architecture. Include domain entities, use cases, interfaces, adapters, and framework boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend module and propose a refactor toward ports and adapters. Identify domain logic, use cases, and infrastructure concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Model A Complex Domain
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Domain-Driven Design model for this business workflow. Define bounded contexts, aggregates, value objects, repositories, and domain events.

output_examples.input: Design an order service with clean boundaries.
output: A domain layer containing Order, Money, Customer, and OrderSubmittedEvent.
Use cases for placing orders, charging payments, and confirming fulfillment.
Ports for order persistence, payment processing, and notifications.
Adapters for PostgreSQL, Stripe, and an email provider.

output_examples.input: Refactor a controller that handles validation, payment, and persistence.
output: Move request handling into a thin controller.
Place business decisions in a use case.
Represent payment and storage behind interfaces.
Test the use case with mock adapters.

output_examples.input: Define DDD building blocks for customer accounts.
output: Email and Money become value objects.
AccountCreated and AddressAdded become domain events.
The repository persists aggregate state without exposing database rows.

best_practices: Keep business rules independent from frameworks, databases, and external services.
Use small interfaces that match actual use-case needs.
Introduce architecture layers when domain complexity justifies the added structure.
anti_patterns: Do not put business decisions inside controllers or framework callbacks.
Do not expose ORM records as domain entities across the application.
Do not apply heavy architecture patterns to simple CRUD workflows without clear need.

faq.question: What architecture patterns does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design for backend systems.

faq.question: Can this skill generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: It provides structures and examples, but production code needs project-specific validation, error handling, and tests.

faq.question: Does it require a specific programming language?
faq.answer: No. The examples use Python, but the architectural guidance applies across backend languages.

faq.question: When should I avoid these patterns?
faq.answer: Avoid them for very small CRUD services where extra layers add more cost than clarity.

faq.question: How does it help with testing?
faq.answer: It separates domain logic from infrastructure, so use cases can be tested with mock ports and adapters.

faq.question: Does it include deployment guidance?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on application architecture, domain modeling, and code organization.
